settings.title: Reconciliation Settings
settings.subtitle: Parameters for the ERP x Acquirer reconciliation process.
settings.schedulingSubtitle: Controls when and how the CardSync automatic pipeline runs.
settings.processingSubtitle: Transaction lookup window for ERP x Acquirer reconciliation.
settings.reconciliationSubtitle: Tolerances and per-step reprocessing for the reconciliation pipeline.
settings.schedulerEnabledHint: Enables or disables automatic pipeline execution. Manual endpoints continue to work even when this flag is off.
settings.completePipelineEnabledHint: Enables the full pipeline run (ERP → Acquirer → Bank → Reconciliation). When disabled the scheduler wakes up but does nothing.
settings.completePipelineCronHint: 6-field Spring cron (seconds minutes hours day month weekday). Changes take effect only after a server restart.
settings.completePipelineStopOnStepErrorHint: When enabled, the pipeline halts subsequent steps if a step throws an exception. Parallel-execution conflicts never stop the pipeline.
settings.logIdleCyclesHint: Emits a debug log each cycle the scheduler wakes up but is disabled. Recommended off in production to avoid log noise.
settings.schedulerSaveError: Could not save the scheduling settings.
settings.erpAcquirerPreviousDaysLookbackHint: Defines how many days before the ERP sale date the acquirer may have recorded the transaction. Set to 0 to require the exact same day.
settings.erpAcquirerFutureDaysLookbackHint: Defines how many days after the ERP sale date the acquirer may have recorded the transaction. Set to 0 to require the exact same day.
settings.reconciliationLookbackMonthsHint: Defines how many months prior to the current date should be searched for transactions during reconciliation. Default: 120 months.
settings.creditOrderPendingDaysHint: Summaries with an RV date older than this number of days and without payment orders are listed as pending for batch creation.
settings.enabledSubtitle: When disabled, the step is skipped (SKIPPED) and the remaining steps continue normally.
settings.reprocessSubtitle: Allows each pipeline step to process already-reconciled records. Use only for audits or targeted corrections — disable after use.
settings.reprocessErpAcquirerSalesHint: Reprocesses ERP sales already reconciled with the acquirer.
settings.reprocessSalesSummaryTransactionsHint: Reprocesses summaries whose transactionsStatus is already reconciled.
settings.reprocessAcquirerSaleCancellationsHint: Reprocesses sales already marked as cancelled by the acquirer.
settings.reprocessErpAcquirerFeesHint: Reprocesses transactions whose feeReconciliationStatus is already reconciled.
settings.reprocessAcquirerSaleSummary: Step 5 — Acquirer Sale x Sales Summary
settings.reprocessAcquirerSaleSummaryHint: Reprocesses summaries whose transactionsStatus is already reconciled.
settings.reprocessSalesSummaryCreditOrder: Step 6 — Sales Summary x Payment Order
settings.reprocessSalesSummaryCreditOrderHint: Reprocesses summaries whose creditOrderStatus is already reconciled.
settings.reprocessBankAcquirer: Step 7 — Payment Order x Bank Statement
settings.reprocessBankAcquirerHint: Reprocesses already-paid credit orders and already-reconciled bank releases.
settings.dateToleranceDaysBeforeHint: Days a bank release can be BEFORE the credit order date. Recommended: 0 (avoids false positives).
settings.dateToleranceDaysAfterHint: Days a bank release can be AFTER the credit order date. Recommended: 5.
settings.valueToleranceHint: Maximum monetary difference tolerated in a reconciliation. E.g. 0.05 accepts up to 5 cents of divergence.
settings.bankMarkNotReconciledAfterDaysHint: Bank releases without a match in the last N days remain pending. After this period, they are marked as unreconciled.
settings.legacyMarkingSubtitle: Controls which bank releases can be manually marked as pre–go-live, based on the release date.
settings.goLiveDateHint: Date the system went live. Used by the reconciliation pipeline to ignore earlier dates and as the reference for the legacy marking window.
settings.legacyMarkingMonthsHint: Only releases dated up to go-live + N months can be marked as legacy. For later releases, the button is hidden in manual reconciliation.

pipeline.description: Runs the full financial reconciliation pipeline in sequence (ERP, Rede, Bank and reconciliations).

stepMessage.ERP_ACQUIRER: ERP vs Acquirer reconciliation complete. Includes reprocessing of manual entries with swapped NSU/authorization. Only reconciled sales here are eligible for fee adjustments.
stepMessage.SALES_SUMMARY_TRANSACTION: Summaries updated based on ADQ transaction status. Summaries without transactions are marked as reconciled.
stepMessage.ACQUIRER_SALE_CANCELLATION: Full cancellations reported by the acquirer were propagated to ADQ and ERP before fees and summaries.
stepMessage.ACQUIRER_SALE_ADJUSTMENTS: Sales with matching or absent (normalized) contract rates advance. Fee divergences block subsequent steps.
stepMessage.ACQUIRER_SALE_SUMMARY: A summary only reconciles when its linked ADQ sales are eligible from previous steps.
stepMessage.SALES_SUMMARY_CREDIT_ORDER: Debit/anticipation without an order may generate a synthetic order for analysis.
stepMessage.CREDIT_ORDER_BANK_RELEASE: A credit order is only eligible when its linked summary is already reconciled.
stepMessage.SKIPPED: Step skipped — no data to process or a previous step was blocked.

reconciliationActions.subtitle: Run each step of the financial reconciliation pipeline individually or the full pipeline at once.

erpPending.description: Sales without business context or contract

bankStatement.subtitle: Analyze bank entries and payment status.

deleteManual.message: Do you want to delete this manual statement?
deleteManual.error: Could not delete the manual statement.

undoReconciliation.message: Undo the reconciliation of this bank release? The linked credit order(s) and installments go back to pending.
undoReconciliation.success: {creditOrdersUnlinked, plural, one {Reconciliation undone: # credit order unlinked.} other {Reconciliation undone: # credit orders unlinked.}}

anticipation.subtitle: Analyze anticipations and respective financial discounts.

managementDashboard.noData: No data available for the selected period.

settings.subtitle: Email sending provider configuration.
settings.implHint: Defines the email provider. FAKE logs emails (development), SMTP uses a configured mail server, BREVO uses the Brevo API.
settings.fromNameHint: Name displayed in the 'From' field of sent emails.
settings.fromEmailHint: Email address displayed in the 'From' field.
settings.brevoApiKeyHint: Authentication key for the Brevo API. Required when the implementation is BREVO.
settings.brevoUsernameHint: Username for Brevo SMTP relay authentication, usually the Brevo account email.
settings.chargebackRecipientsHint: Emails that will receive a notification when chargebacks are detected during file processing. Separate multiple addresses with a comma.
settings.smtpUsernameHint: Username for SMTP authentication, usually the email address itself.
settings.smtpAuthHint: Enables SMTP authentication with username and password.
settings.smtpStarttlsHint: Enables connection upgrade to TLS using STARTTLS. Recommended for port 587.
settings.smtpSslHint: Enables direct SSL/TLS connection. Recommended for port 465.

status.title: Scheduler Status
status.subtitle: Track the last automatic/manual execution of ERP, Rede and Bank processing.

upload.subtitle: Manually upload ERP, acquirer or bank files for processing.
upload.dropHint: Accepts one or more files at a time.

browser.subtitle: Browse and download files stored in the processing volume.
browser.empty: No files found in this folder.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.hint: If you arrived here through a system link, review the route registration or the corresponding menu item.
notFound.message: The informed route does not exist or has been removed. Check the accessed address or go back to the dashboard.
